name: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 (W3C Recommendation, 2023)
summary: The W3C accessibility guidelines organized under four principles (Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust — 'POUR'), with testable success criteria at conformance levels A, AA and AAA. Serves as the accessibility coverage oracle — whether requirements state an accessibility conformance target and address each POUR principle.

leaves.name: Perceivable
leaves.description: Information and UI components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive — text alternatives, captions, adaptable and distinguishable content (color contrast, resize).

leaves.name: Operable
leaves.description: UI components and navigation must be operable — keyboard accessible, enough time, no seizures, navigable, and 2.2 additions like target size and dragging alternatives.

leaves.name: Understandable
leaves.description: Information and operation of the UI must be understandable — readable, predictable, and input assistance (labels, error identification, consistent help).

leaves.name: Robust
leaves.description: Content must be robust enough to be interpreted reliably by user agents and assistive technologies — valid markup, name/role/value, status messages.

leaves.name: Conformance level (A / AA / AAA)
leaves.description: A stated WCAG conformance target — Level A (minimum), AA (typical legal/procurement baseline), or AAA (highest) — that scopes which success criteria apply.
